description: Baseline run completed but metric parser failed on ANSI-colored vitest summaries

asi.hypothesis: Establish full 1.0 sweep baseline after fixing SurrealDB and harness setup.
asi.error: Suites actually ran, but autoresearch.sh parsed all failures as 999 because Vitest summary lines contain ANSI escape codes between numbers and words.
asi.observed_true_counts: query-presets 0 failed/11 passed; trash 0 failed/97 passed; uploads 4 failed/98 passed; joins 28 failed/46 passed/1 skipped; queues 71 failed/1 passed/2 skipped.
asi.rollback_reason: Invalid metric extraction, not an adapter regression.
asi.next_action_hint: Strip ANSI escape codes before summary parsing, then rerun baseline. Expected valid primary baseline around 103 failures if same results reproduce.

asi.hypothesis: Use a broad objective 1.0 blocker metric across green guardrails plus remaining uploads/joins/queues suites.
asi.baseline: blocker_failures=103 = uploads 4 + joins 28 + queues 71; query-presets and trash are green.
asi.next_action_hint: Largest objective win is queues: failures stem from seed duplicate email before most queue tests run. Fixing payload_jobs/deleteMany/drop/reset behavior may unlock ~70 tests quickly. Joins next largest; uploads has 2 env paste-url failures plus 2 stale file collision failures.

asi.hypothesis: Giving the adapter a drizzle._.schema would let Payload resetDB delete all tables before queue seeding, eliminating duplicate user failures.
asi.rollback_reason: Worse primary metric: queues went from 71 to 72 failures. The helper then treated the adapter as full Drizzle and createDrizzleSnapshot crashed on missing db.drizzle.query[tableName].fullSchema.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not expose a populated drizzle._.schema unless also implementing enough drizzle.query/select snapshot surface. Better options: implement clearDatabase and avoid 'drizzle' branch, or enhance the existing drizzle shim to support snapshot APIs.

description: Implement fuller drizzle snapshot shim for queue reset

asi.hypothesis: A minimal drizzle query/select/insert shim plus schema would let Payload clearAndSeed reset and snapshot queue fixtures correctly.
asi.rollback_reason: Still worse than baseline. resetDB attempted DELETE FROM posts_rels from the exposed schema, but that table was registered in adapter metadata without a matching DEFINE TABLE, causing reset errors and duplicate seed failures.
asi.next_action_hint: If retrying this path, either define every table exposed in drizzle._.schema (including *_rels/locales) or only expose actually-defined tables. Avoid partial schema entries that resetDB will delete.

description: Add reset/snapshot-compatible drizzle shim for queue fixtures

asi.hypothesis: Expose a minimal but complete drizzle-compatible schema/query/select/insert shim and define all metadata tables so Payload clearAndSeed can reset and snapshot queue fixtures.
asi.result: queues improved from 71 failed / 1 passed to 35 failed / 37 passed; full 1.0 blocker metric improved from 103 to 67.
asi.learned: Queue failures were substantially caused by reset/snapshot incompatibility, not only updateJobs runtime behavior. Defining all metadata tables exposed in the shim avoids DELETE missing-table errors.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue queues: inspect remaining 35 failures. Likely now true job lifecycle/updateJobs/concurrency semantics rather than seed reset.

description: Normalize drizzle snapshot records before restore

asi.hypothesis: Queue login failures after snapshot restore were caused by raw Surreal record IDs in drizzle snapshot rows, so restored users could not be found by Payload string ID.
asi.result: queues improved from 35 failed / 37 passed to 11 failed / 61 passed; full blocker metric improved from 67 to 43.
asi.learned: Snapshot rows exposed through the drizzle shim must be normalized with normalizeDocument before caching/restoring, otherwise IDs become record objects and auth lookups fail after restore.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect remaining 11 queue failures. They likely concern true queue execution semantics: updateJobs limit/sort, deleteJobOnComplete, retries, cancellation, concurrency/supersedes, or nested task status updates.

description: Try cleaning generated upload files before uploads suite

asi.hypothesis: Cleaning stale generated upload files before the uploads suite would remove filename collision failures and reduce blocker_failures by 2.
asi.rollback_reason: The cleanup pattern was too broad and deleted fixture file animated.webp, causing uploads onInit to fail and uploads_failures=999.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not broad-delete test/uploads files. If addressing uploads noise, only delete known generated static directories or use Payload's seedDB uploadsDir cleanup; first restore fixtures with git checkout in the Payload repo.

description: Treat adapter-populated join fields as non-localized for afterRead

asi.hypothesis: Payload afterRead was stripping or hoisting adapter-populated join pagination objects when the sanitized join field inherited localized=true from its target relationship; marking join fields non-localized should preserve populated join result shapes.
asi.result: joins improved from 28 failed / 46 passed to 25 failed / 49 passed; uploads also improved by one noisy file-collision failure; full blocker metric improved from 43 to 39.
asi.learned: For this adapter, join result objects are already locale-resolved and should not be treated as localized field storage by Payload's afterRead recursion.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue joins: remaining failures still include collection-array sorting/query shape, access/filter/pagination, and top-level where-by-join timeouts. Consider implementing join-aware where prefilter instead of allowing recursive population loops.

description: Add join page metadata and slicing

asi.hypothesis: Implementing page/start slicing and page metadata for join fields would reduce REST/GraphQL join pagination failures.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from 39 to 40. Joins improved by 1 (25→24 failures), but queues regressed by 1 and uploads noisy failures increased by 1, so under full 1.0 rubric this is not a keep.
asi.next_action_hint: The pagination change is promising for joins and may be worth reapplying later with a queues guard or after stabilizing noisy uploads/queues. It likely fixed one join pagination case but must not be kept alone under current primary metric.

description: Support nested object join options lookup

asi.hypothesis: Some nested join failures may come from Payload passing joins as nested objects instead of dot-path keys, so resolving join options by walking path segments would restore sort/limit/page for group joins.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from best 39 to 41. Joins did not improve versus best and queues/uploads were noisier in this full sweep.
asi.next_action_hint: Nested join options lookup alone is not sufficient; group join sort failure likely comes from Payload afterRead repopulation or adapter result shape rather than missing joinOptions. Investigate targeted join logs before retrying.

description: Clean ignored upload artifacts before uploads conformance sweep

asi.hypothesis: The remaining non-environment upload failures in the full sweep were caused by ignored/generated upload artifacts from prior runs, so cleaning ignored files in test/uploads before the uploads suite should produce clean-env upload results without deleting tracked fixtures.
asi.result: uploads improved from 3 failed / 99 passed at best to 2 failed / 100 passed; blocker_failures improved from 39 to 38. The two remaining upload failures are the known localhost/nginx paste-url status expectations.
asi.learned: Use git clean -fdX scoped to test/uploads, not broad file deletion. This removes ignored generated artifacts while preserving tracked fixture files such as animated.webp.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with true adapter blockers: joins 25 failures and queues 11 failures. Uploads can be considered clean-env green except for the documented local nginx paste-url issue.

description: Use Surreal atomic append for top-level $push updates

asi.hypothesis: Queue parallel task log loss was caused by read-merge-write races for top-level $push updates, so translating $push into Surreal atomic array append should preserve concurrent log entries.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from 38 to 60; queues regressed from 11 to 33 failures. Atomic append via SET log += [item] caused updateJob calls to return null / jobs to lose expected log state in queue execution paths.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use naive Surreal += for $push. If revisiting concurrent log updates, test Surreal array semantics in isolation and ensure UPDATE returns the updated full document. A safer path may be transactional compare/merge or dedicated log sub-records, but avoid changing this without targeted queue tests first.

description: Use lexicographic join sorting to match Payload string sort semantics

asi.hypothesis: Several join sort failures were caused by adapter-side join sorting using numeric string collation, which orders titles like 'test 14' after 'test 9' differently than Payload/DB lexicographic string sort expectations.
asi.result: joins improved from 25 failed / 49 passed to 21 failed / 53 passed; full blocker metric improved from 38 to 35 despite one noisy queue failure regression.
asi.learned: Join field sorting should use plain lexicographic string comparison, not numeric collation. Numeric-aware sorting overfits human-natural ordering and breaks Payload's expected string order for titles with embedded numbers.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue joins. Remaining failures now likely include localized/versioned joins, collection-array explicit sort/filter, access filters, and top-level where-by-join timeouts.

description: Honor join page offsets and pagination metadata

asi.hypothesis: After lexicographic sorting stabilized join order, implementing adapter-side join page offsets and metadata should fix REST join pagination without the earlier noisy regressions.
asi.result: joins improved from 21 failed / 53 passed to 20 failed / 54 passed; full blocker metric improved from 35 to 33.
asi.learned: Join pagination needs start=(page-1)*limit slicing plus hasPrevPage/hasNextPage/page/pagingCounter metadata. This is now a real improvement when combined with correct string sort semantics.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue joins. Remaining failures include localized/versioned joins, collection-array explicit sort/filter, access filtering, and top-level where-by-join timeouts.
